name: [CSSLint v1][06.8] Preserve deferred plugin boundary
description: Keep `no_unused_scoped_selectors` out of core v1 defaults and document plugin-path contract references. Done when rule remains deferred and extension path is explicit in docs and config behavior.
result: Locked deferred plugin boundary for no_unused_scoped_selectors by enforcing core rule-id allowlisting in csslint-config (unknown IDs now fail fast), adding tests for deferred rule rejection, and clarifying docs that this rule stays outside core v1 config until plugin loading exists.

name: [CSSLint v1][08.3] Implement preset and target resolution
description: Expand `recommended`, `strict`, and `minimal` presets into resolved rule maps and pass target settings to parser/rules. Done when CLI/config precedence is deterministic and documented.
result: Implemented canonical preset and target resolution: recommended/strict/minimal now expand to explicit rule maps in deterministic canonical order, and per-rule overrides apply after preset expansion. Added target profile parsing with v1 defaults and CLI precedence (--targets over config targets over defaults), then threaded resolved targets into parser options and rule execution context via run_rules_with_config_and_targets. Updated docs/plan/08-cli-and-config.md to document effective precedence. Verified with cargo test -p csslint-core -p csslint-config -p csslint-parser -p csslint-rules -p csslint-cli.

name: [CSSLint v1][02] Workspace and crate layout
description:
## Goal
Stand up a Rust workspace with strict crate boundaries and one-way dependencies.

## Source Docs
- docs/plan/02-workspace-and-crate-layout.md

## Plan
1. Create workspace crates: core, extractor, parser, semantic, rules, fix, config, cli, test-harness (+ optional plugin-api).
2. Implement shared core contracts (`FileId`, `Span`, `Diagnostic`, `Fix`, `RuleId`, `Severity`, `Scope`).
3. Enforce dependency direction (`core -> extractor/parser -> semantic -> rules/fix -> cli`) and prevent cycles.
4. Set CI baseline (`check`, `lint`, `unit`, `integration`, `perf`) with pinned toolchain and strict linting.
5. Reach end-to-end smoke path for one CSS file and one diagnostic.

## Done When
- `cargo check --workspace` passes with all crates present.
- Dependency direction rules are enforced and documented.
- CI lanes are configured for build, lint, tests, and perf smoke.
- Architecture boundaries are accepted as v1-final.
result: Completed workspace-and-layout foundation: scaffolded all planned crates with ownership docs, implemented canonical core contracts and cross-crate typed APIs, enforced one-way dependency policy with parser-only lightning boundary checks, and established pinned toolchain plus baseline CI lanes for check/lint/unit/integration/perf.

name: [CSSLint v1][08.5] Implement schema-stable JSON reporter
description: Emit schemaVersion 1 payloads with required summary/diagnostics/internalErrors/timing fields and always-present fix object. Done when fixtures validate against docs/json-output-schema-v1.schema.json.
result: Implemented schemaVersion 1 JSON reporter output in the CLI with required top-level envelope fields (schemaVersion/tool/summary/diagnostics/internalErrors/timing), always-present fix objects, and summary counts including exitCode. Added phase timing and duration tracking in lint execution and updated JSON serialization tests to assert schema shape. Verified with cargo test -p csslint-cli and schema fixture validation via a temporary virtualenv: /tmp/csslint-jsonschema-venv/bin/python scripts/validate_json_output_schema.py.

name: [CSSLint roadmap] Deferred items from v1 scope lock
description:
## Goal
Convert docs/deferrals-v1.md into tracked post-v1 backlog so scope pressure is visible without destabilizing v1 delivery.

## Source Docs
- docs/deferrals-v1.md
- docs/plugin-surface-v1.md
- docs/template-usage-provider-spec-v1.md

## Plan
Track each deferral as an owned roadmap task with acceptance criteria, milestone intent (v1.5/v2), and impact notes.

## Done When
- Every deferral D-001..D-008 exists as a dex child task with owner placeholder and milestone target.
- Changes to scope update both docs and dex backlog.

name: [CSSLint v1][09.2] Build fixture importer and translator
description: Translate Stylelint JS test cases into native fixture schema with rule id, expected diagnostics, spans, and optional fix output. Done when importer output is deterministic and reviewable.
result: Implemented a deterministic Stylelint fixture importer at scripts/import_stylelint_fixtures.mjs that evaluates curated JS testRule snapshots and emits native JSON fixtures with mapped csslint rule IDs, expected diagnostics (severity/message_contains/line/column), and optional fixed output. Added pinned upstream snapshot inputs under tests/compat/stylelint/upstream and generated reviewable fixtures under tests/compat/stylelint/imported with a --check drift mode for reproducibility.

name: [Deferral D-002] Expand toward full Stylelint compatibility (v2)
description: Evaluate parity gaps that require PostCSS/custom syntax behavior and define bounded compatibility roadmap. Done when high-cost parity items are explicitly prioritized and risked.

name: [CSSLint v1][06.6] Enforce required rule-test matrix coverage
description: Use docs/plan/rule-test-matrix.md as a hard gate across css/vue/svelte, imported/native, and lint/fix modes. Done when every required matrix cell has passing tests.
result: Added a rule-test-matrix gate test (crates/csslint-test-harness/tests/rule_test_matrix_gate.rs) that enforces required report/no-report expectations across imported CSS, native CSS, Vue, and Svelte contexts, and verifies fix availability for all fixable rules in required contexts. Verified with cargo test -p csslint-test-harness --test rule_test_matrix_gate and full cargo test -p csslint-test-harness.

name: [CSSLint v1][03.5] Harden malformed input handling
description: Guarantee extractor does not panic on malformed component sources and reports controlled diagnostics. Done when malformed corpus runs cleanly with no crashes.
result: Hardened extractor malformed-input behavior by emitting controlled extractor_unclosed_script_tag diagnostics for malformed script sections, and added a malformed corpus under tests/native/extractor-malformed with integration coverage (catch_unwind guard) to ensure malformed Vue/Svelte inputs never panic and always report controlled diagnostics.

name: [CSSLint v1][02.2] Implement canonical core type contracts
description: Define shared `FileId`, `Span`, `Diagnostic`, `Fix`, `RuleId`, `Severity`, and `Scope` in csslint-core and adopt them across crate boundaries. Done when cross-crate conversion shims are unnecessary.
result: Implemented canonical core contracts (FileId, Span, RuleId, Severity, Scope, Fix, Diagnostic) and rewired crate APIs to use shared types directly across extractor, parser, semantic, rules, fix, config, cli, and test-harness without conversion shims.

name: [CSSLint v1][06.1] Implement Wave 1 fixable quick wins
description: Implement `no_empty_rules`, `no_duplicate_declarations`, and `no_legacy_vendor_prefixes` with conservative safe fixes. Done when wave 1 tests pass and fixes are idempotent.
result: Implemented Wave 1 rules in core registry: no_empty_rules, no_duplicate_declarations, and no_legacy_vendor_prefixes with conservative fixes and semantic-aware rewrite safety. Added cross-framework idempotency coverage in csslint-test-harness (CSS/Vue/Svelte) and all workspace tests pass.

name: [CSSLint v1][03] Extractor and source mapping
description:
## Goal
Implement offset-accurate extraction for `.css`, `.vue`, and `.svelte` with deterministic diagnostics on malformed input.

## Source Docs
- docs/plan/03-extractor-and-source-mapping.md
- docs/vue-style-policy-v1.md

## Plan
1. Build O(n) byte scanner for style extraction with robust quoted-attribute handling.
2. Implement framework-specific block metadata (`scoped`, `module`, `framework`, offsets).
3. Apply Vue policies: unsupported `lang` => error and skip; `<style src>` => warning and skip.
4. Build line-index map for LF/CRLF and stable offset->line/column conversion.
5. Add fixtures for malformed blocks, multi-style files, edge offsets, and large files.

## Done When
- Extractor outputs correct block metadata and original-file byte spans.
- Mapping assertions pass for `.css`, `.vue`, and `.svelte`.
- Malformed files never panic and yield controlled diagnostics.
- Vue `<style module>` and `<style src>` behavior matches policy docs.
result: Completed extractor and source-mapping epic: implemented O(n) byte-level extraction with deterministic block ordering and offsets for CSS/Vue/Svelte, enforced Vue style policy for src/lang/module/scoped metadata, added LF/CRLF offset-first mapping utilities, built fixture corpus with mapping/order assertions, and hardened malformed-input handling with controlled diagnostics and no-panic malformed corpus coverage.

name: [CSSLint v1][11.5] Validate determinism under parallel execution
description: Run identical workloads across thread counts and assert byte-identical diagnostics and fix outputs. Done when determinism checks are green across mixed file type corpora.

name: [CSSLint v1][03.1] Build byte-level style extractor
description: Implement O(n) extraction for `.css`, Vue `<style>` blocks, and Svelte `<style>` blocks with ordered block indexes and offsets. Done when extraction is deterministic and preserves original byte positions.
result: Implemented an O(n) byte-level extractor for CSS, Vue, and Svelte with deterministic style block ordering, block indexes, and original-file byte offsets/start-end spans; introduced typed extraction output contract (framework/lang/scoped/module metadata) and rewired parser/semantic/rules/cli/tests to consume extracted block metadata directly.

name: [CSSLint v1][01.2] Finalize presets and severity defaults
description: Use docs/rule-catalog-v1.md to freeze recommended/strict/minimal preset maps and per-rule default severities. Done when preset expansion is canonical and config validation behavior is defined for unknown ids/severities.
result: Finalized v1 preset/severity behavior in docs/rule-catalog-v1.md by adding a canonical preset expansion contract, override order, deterministic rule ordering, and explicit config_error handling for unknown IDs and invalid severities.

name: [CSSLint v1][08.1] Implement CLI command surface and discovery
description: Implement `csslint <path>`, `--fix`, and `--format json` flows with include set `.css/.vue/.svelte` and default ignores. Done when CLI traverses mixed projects deterministically.
result: Implemented the v1 CLI command surface for csslint path, --fix, and --format json|pretty; replaced scaffold wiring with full extraction-to-rules execution over discovered files. Added deterministic target discovery for .css/.vue/.svelte with default ignores (node_modules, build dirs, VCS dirs) and stable sorting, plus unit coverage for argument parsing and discovery determinism. Verified with cargo test -p csslint-cli.

name: [CSSLint v1][10.5] Enforce component-file fix safety and CI integration
description: Verify fixes apply only inside style regions and remain idempotent in component files; integrate suite into CI blocking gates. Done when no template/script corruption occurs in fix mode.

name: [CSSLint v1][06.7] Validate fix idempotency for fixable rules
description: Run repeated `--fix` cycles for fixable rules across framework file types and ensure second pass is a no-op. Done when idempotency suite is green and regressions are blocked.
result: Added an all-fixable idempotency harness test covering no_empty_rules, no_duplicate_declarations, no_legacy_vendor_prefixes, and prefer_logical_properties across CSS, Vue, and Svelte inputs. The suite applies fixes twice and asserts second pass no-op and byte-identical output. Verified with cargo test -p csslint-test-harness --test fix_idempotency_fixable_rules --test wave1_fixable_rules --test wave4_framework_rules.

name: [CSSLint v1][07.2] Implement deterministic overlap resolver
description: Resolve conflicting edits using severity, priority, shorter-span, and rule-id tie-breakers. Done when overlap resolution is stable and repeatable across runs.
result: Added deterministic overlap resolution to csslint-fix. Candidate fixes are ranked by severity, priority, span length, and rule ID; conflicting lower-ranked fixes are dropped with explicit overlap reason. Added unit tests for each tie-break level and input-order independence.

name: [Deferral D-008] Resolve Vue style src external files (v1.5)
description: Plan safe external style resolution for Vue SFC style src blocks with deterministic IO and diagnostics. Done when extraction strategy handles path resolution and caching explicitly.

name: [CSSLint v1][08.4] Implement human-readable pretty reporter
description: Add concise diagnostic output with stable ordering, optional code-frame behavior, and summary counts. Done when pretty output is deterministic across repeated runs.
result: Implemented a deterministic human-readable pretty reporter with grouped file sections, concise diagnostic rows, and stable summary counts. Added optional code-frame output via --code-frame, sourced from per-file snapshots captured during linting, and validated deterministic rendering with dedicated CLI tests. Verified with cargo test -p csslint-cli.

name: [CSSLint v1][01.3] Lock JSON report and exit code contracts
description: Use docs/json-output-schema-v1.schema.json, docs/json-output-schema-v1.md, and docs/open-questions.md to lock schemaVersion=1 fields plus exit-code precedence 0/1/2 semantics. Done when machine schema and human docs are aligned.
result: Aligned JSON report contract docs by adding a normative exit-code precedence section in docs/json-output-schema-v1.md, embedding matching exitCode semantics in docs/json-output-schema-v1.schema.json, and linking the resolved decision in docs/open-questions.md.

name: [CSSLint v1][09.4] Wire compat-fast and compat-full CI lanes
description: Run curated compatibility checks on PRs and full imported subsets on main/nightly without blocking on known deferred cases. Done when lanes are stable and integrated into CI status reporting.
result: Wired dedicated compatibility CI lanes: compat-fast now runs on pull requests with deterministic importer drift checks, skip-manifest governance validation, and curated fast compatibility execution; compat-full runs on main pushes and nightly schedule with full imported-suite execution while honoring explicit skip-manifest deferrals. Added a new stylelint_compat_harness integration test (fast + full) and updated success-metrics lane mapping to reflect compat-fast/compat-full ownership.

name: [CSSLint v1][03.2] Implement Vue style policy behaviors
description: Support Vue attributes `scoped`, `module`, `module="name"`, `lang`, and `src` per docs/vue-style-policy-v1.md. Done when unsupported lang emits error+skip and `<style src>` emits warning+skip (including src+inline cases).
result: Implemented Vue style policy behavior in extractor: style blocks with src now emit unsupported_external_style_src warning and are skipped (including src plus inline blocks), unsupported style lang emits unsupported_style_lang error and skips the block, and module/module=name attributes are preserved as module metadata for extracted blocks.

name: [CSSLint v1][10.2] Encode scope truth table and mixed-scope cases
description: Assert default scope behavior for css/vue/svelte contexts and mixed global escapes in selectors. Done when :global full and partial escapes behave exactly as documented.
result: Added native scope truth-table coverage with fixture-driven tests for Vue scoped/module/plain and Svelte scoped defaults, including full and partial :global escapes. Added css default-global assertion and validated scope index counts/selector part scopes via csslint-test-harness native_scope_truth_table.

name: [CSSLint v1][11] Performance and hardening gates
description:
## Goal
Protect the core value proposition with benchmark reporting, regression budgets, fuzzing, malformed-input reliability, and determinism under parallelism.

## Source Docs
- docs/plan/11-performance-and-hardening.md
- docs/success-metrics.md

## Plan
1. Build benchmark harness across CSS-only, Vue-heavy, Svelte-heavy, and mixed corpora.
2. Record runtime/throughput/latency/memory/phase timings and publish comparative Stylelint ratios.
3. Enforce regression budgets (runtime and peak memory) in CI with explicit override rationale policy.
4. Add fuzz targets (extractor, parser wrapper, semantic builder) and malformed corpus reliability lane.
5. Run determinism validation across thread counts and mixed input sets.
6. Add optional profiling outputs for slow files/rules behind debug flag.

## Done When
- Benchmark and compatibility artifacts are produced for release candidates.
- No critical panics remain in fuzz or malformed corpus lanes.
- Deterministic diagnostics/fixes hold across parallel execution settings.
- Performance regressions over budget fail CI unless explicitly accepted with rationale.

name: [CSSLint v1][11.3] Enforce perf regression budgets in CI
description: Fail performance lane on median runtime or peak memory regressions beyond budget unless explicit rationale override is documented. Done when budget checks gate merges reliably.

name: [CSSLint v1][07.4] Build overlap and idempotency test suites
description: Add unit/integration/property tests for overlap matrices, CRLF/Unicode offsets, and repeated `--fix` no-op behavior. Done when all fixable rules satisfy idempotency contract.
result: Expanded fix-engine test coverage with a new integration suite for overlap tie-break matrices, CRLF+Unicode offset safety, and repeated fix no-op behavior. Updated existing wave1/full-fix idempotency harness tests to run the full fix pipeline (collect, resolve, apply) rather than raw fix vectors so rejection/conflict logic is exercised.

name: [CSSLint v1][04.4] Encode framework scope annotation rules
description: Apply scope defaults for css/vue/svelte and `:global(...)` overrides at selector-part granularity. Done when mixed-scope selectors preserve explicit global escapes and scoped defaults correctly.
result: Encoded framework scope annotations at selector-part granularity: selector tokenization now preserves default block scope (CSS global, Vue scoped/module scoped context, Svelte scoped) while extracting :global(...) segments as explicit global parts; selectors_by_scope index now records mixed-scope selectors in all participating scope buckets with dedicated tests for Vue scoped plus global override behavior.

name: [CSSLint v1][06.3] Implement Wave 3 controlled-complexity rules
description: Implement `no_invalid_values` (high-confidence v1 subset) and `no_deprecated_features` (target-aware policy). Done when conservative validation avoids noisy false positives and fixtures pass.
result: Implemented Wave 3 rules: no_invalid_values as a conservative high-confidence subset (keyword-only checks for display/position/visibility/box-sizing/overflow/flex-direction/flex-wrap plus opacity range) and no_deprecated_features with v1-baseline policy checks for deprecated at-rules/properties/display values. Added rule-unit coverage and a new wave3 harness suite across imported CSS plus native Vue/Svelte cases. Updated rule specs and verified with cargo test -p csslint-rules -p csslint-test-harness.

name: [CSSLint v1][03.4] Build extractor fixture corpus
description: Add unit/integration fixtures for multi-block Vue/Svelte files, empty style tags, missing closing tags, and embedded style-like text. Done when mapping and block-order assertions pass.
result: Added extractor fixture corpus under tests/native/extractor for Vue multi-block ordering, Svelte extraction, empty style tags, missing closing style tags, and style-like text inside script; added integration test crates/csslint-test-harness/tests/extractor_fixtures.rs to assert block order and offset mapping via source slice checks plus expected diagnostic contracts.

name: [CSSLint v1][11.4] Add fuzz targets and malformed corpus lanes
description: Add fuzzers for extractor/parser wrapper/semantic builder and run malformed input reliability suites. Done when panic-free behavior is demonstrated and failures are triaged deterministically.

name: [CSSLint v1][02.3] Enforce one-way dependency direction
description: Ensure internal dependencies follow `core -> extractor/parser -> semantic -> rules/fix -> cli`, with parser as only Lightning integration point. Done when no cycles or forbidden imports remain.
result: Enforced one-way workspace dependency policy with scripts/check_internal_deps.py (forbidden edge detection, cycle detection, and parser-only lightningcss rule), documented the enforcement command in docs/plan/02-workspace-and-crate-layout.md, and reserved Lightning integration to csslint-parser via an optional lightningcss dependency.

name: [CSSLint v1][06.5] Keep per-rule specs and divergences current
description: Maintain docs/rules/*.md with intent, algorithm, defaults, fix safety, and divergence notes for each rule. Done when implementation and docs stay in lockstep with no undocumented behavior.
result: Audited and updated per-rule specs to match implemented behavior for wave rules and current parser constraints. Aligned no_duplicate_selectors, no_duplicate_declarations, no_overqualified_selectors, and no_deprecated_features docs with actual algorithms/divergences, and clarified no_global_leaks matrix semantics in docs/plan/rule-test-matrix.md (plain CSS covered as explicit no-report assertion).

name: [Deferral D-003] Support stylelint-disable directive comments (v1.5)
description: Design suppression comment model compatible with csslint config/runtime guarantees. Done when directive parsing semantics and reporting impacts are specified and testable.

name: [CSSLint v1][03.3] Add offset-first line/column mapper
description: Build reusable line-start index supporting LF and CRLF and binary-search offset mapping. Done when local spans map back to global file spans via start_offset formula with boundary tests.
result: Added reusable offset-first mapping utilities in csslint-core: a LineIndex with LF/CRLF-aware line-start indexing plus binary-search offset-to-line/column conversion, and a map_local_span_to_global helper implementing the start_offset + local_offset formula with boundary-focused tests.

name: [CSSLint v1][09.1] Pin Stylelint source and suite map
description: Choose and pin one Stylelint commit SHA, then map imported suites to local rule IDs and supported option subsets. Done when source pinning and suite coverage are reproducible.
result: Pinned Stylelint compatibility source to commit 5bd2d21e8a6b47f529314284d162e6dcb37ef681 and added a reproducible suite map covering the eight selected imported suites with explicit local-rule mappings and v1-supported option subsets. Added compatibility corpus README and linked implementation file locations in the Step 9 plan doc.

name: [CSSLint v1][11.1] Build benchmark corpora and harness
description: Create benchmark inputs for css-only, vue-heavy, svelte-heavy, and mixed repositories with warm/cold protocol metadata. Done when harness can run reproducibly across corpora.

name: [CSSLint v1][01] Scope lock and release criteria
description:
## Goal
Lock v1 scope, defaults, and release gates so implementation cannot drift.

## Source Docs
- docs/plan/01-scope-and-success-criteria.md
- docs/rule-catalog-v1.md
- docs/open-questions.md
- docs/deferrals-v1.md
- docs/success-metrics.md

## Plan
1. Freeze v1 in-scope and non-goals, including parser and plugin boundaries.
2. Confirm the canonical 10-rule catalog, tags, severities, and preset memberships.
3. Lock JSON reporter contract and exit-code precedence decisions.
4. Map each release gate (correctness, compatibility, framework, performance, determinism) to CI lanes.
5. Define deferral process for any post-v1 request.

## Done When
- Rule catalog defaults and severity policy are final.
- All decisions in docs/open-questions.md remain resolved.
- Release gates are mapped to concrete CI checks and artifact expectations.
- Deferral workflow is documented and used for out-of-scope requests.
result: Completed scope-lock tranche by freezing v1 scope boundaries/rule tags, finalizing preset and severity contracts, aligning JSON schema/reporter exit-code semantics, and mapping every release gate to a concrete CI lane with blocking criteria and artifacts.

name: [CSSLint v1][05] Deterministic rule engine
description:
## Goal
Implement a typed, deterministic, single-traversal rule engine with safe rule isolation and stable sorting.

## Source Docs
- docs/plan/05-rule-engine.md
- docs/plugin-surface-v1.md
- docs/template-usage-provider-spec-v1.md

## Plan
1. Implement rule registry with stable rule-id ordering and typed visitor callbacks.
2. Run enabled rules in one semantic traversal with precomputed event subscriptions.
3. Validate rule configs (`off|warn|error`) before traversal and surface config diagnostics early.
4. Contain rule runtime failures without crashing full lint runs.
5. Sort diagnostics globally by deterministic key after parallel file processing.
6. Keep extension hooks for compile-time rule packs and optional providers.

## Done When
- First rule batch runs through one-pass dispatch only.
- Determinism snapshots are stable across repeated runs and thread counts.
- Rule config errors are surfaced before linting execution.
- Extension boundaries exist without weakening safety guarantees.
result: Completed deterministic rule-engine epic: introduced typed Rule/RuleMeta/RuleVisitor contracts and deterministic registry ordering, implemented single-pass semantic dispatch with subscriber precomputation, added config preflight validation plus severity override plumbing, enforced deterministic diagnostic merge/sort keys, and added panic containment with compile-time rule-pack/provider extension hooks while keeping full cargo fmt/clippy/check/test suite green.

name: [CSSLint v1][05.4] Enforce deterministic merge and sorting
description: Sort diagnostics by file path, span, severity, rule id, and message after parallel file execution. Done when repeated runs produce byte-identical output ordering.
result: Added deterministic diagnostic ordering utilities to csslint-rules with a shared stable sort key (file id, span start/end, severity, rule id, message), integrated sorting into rule execution output, and added merge-and-sort support for multi-batch/parallel aggregation with regression tests for repeatable ordering.

name: [CSSLint v1][09.3] Maintain explicit skip manifest governance
description: Track skipped cases with reason codes (custom_syntax, scss_less, directive_comments, postcss_integration, unsupported_option) and notes. Done when all skips are intentional and counted.
result: Added an explicit skip manifest at tests/compat/stylelint/skip-manifest.yaml with reason-coded entries (custom_syntax, scss_less, directive_comments, postcss_integration, unsupported_option), notes, and linked deferral IDs. Added scripts/validate_stylelint_skip_manifest.py to enforce manifest governance by validating entry-to-fixture mapping, reason-code validity, embedded skip metadata parity, and deterministic per-reason skip counts.

name: [CSSLint v1][01.4] Map release gates to CI lanes
description: Map correctness, compatibility, framework, performance, and determinism gates to concrete CI jobs and artifacts per docs/success-metrics.md. Done when every gate has an owning lane and blocking criteria.
result: Mapped all v1 release gates in docs/success-metrics.md to concrete CI lanes with explicit blocking criteria and required artifacts so correctness, compatibility, framework, performance, and determinism all have clear ownership.

name: [CSSLint v1][02.4] Establish toolchain and CI baseline lanes
description: Pin Rust toolchain and wire CI lanes for check, fmt/clippy, unit, integration, and perf smoke. Done when baseline lanes pass on workspace skeleton and fail appropriately on violations.
result: Pinned Rust toolchain via rust-toolchain.toml (1.82.0 with rustfmt/clippy), added a Rust CI workflow with baseline lanes (check, lint, unit, integration, perf), wired lint lane to run the internal dependency policy script, and added unit/integration/perf smoke tests to give each lane executable coverage on the workspace skeleton.

name: [CSSLint v1][06] Rule implementation waves (10 rules)
description:
## Goal
Deliver all 10 v1 rules in phased waves with fixture-matrix coverage and per-rule specs.

## Source Docs
- docs/plan/06-first-rule-batch.md
- docs/plan/rule-test-matrix.md
- docs/rule-catalog-v1.md
- docs/rules/README.md
- docs/rules/*.md

## Plan
1. Implement Wave 1 fixable quick wins: `no_empty_rules`, `no_duplicate_declarations`, `no_legacy_vendor_prefixes`.
2. Implement Wave 2 compatibility priority rules: `no_duplicate_selectors`, `no_unknown_properties`, `no_overqualified_selectors`.
3. Implement Wave 3 controlled complexity: `no_invalid_values` subset and `no_deprecated_features` policy checks.
4. Implement Wave 4 framework-native rules: `no_global_leaks`, `prefer_logical_properties`.
5. Keep per-rule docs, divergences, tests, and fix-idempotency evidence in lockstep.

## Done When
- All 10 v1 rule IDs are implemented exactly as cataloged.
- Every required matrix cell in docs/plan/rule-test-matrix.md is green.
- Fixable rules are idempotent across css/vue/svelte paths where required.
- Deferred `no_unused_scoped_selectors` remains out of core defaults and documented as plugin-path.
result: Completed all wave tasks for v1 rule implementation: wave1 fixable rules (no_empty_rules, no_duplicate_declarations, no_legacy_vendor_prefixes), wave2 compatibility rules (no_duplicate_selectors, no_unknown_properties, no_overqualified_selectors), wave3 controlled rules (no_invalid_values subset, no_deprecated_features baseline policy), and wave4 native rules (prefer_logical_properties with safe fixes, no_global_leaks scoped leak detection). Added per-wave harness suites, a rule-matrix gate, and full fix idempotency coverage across CSS/Vue/Svelte; docs/specs and divergence notes are now aligned.

name: [CSSLint v1][10.3] Validate Vue module and src policy behavior
description: Test Vue style module as scoped-context and style src warning+skip behavior, including src plus inline content edge case. Done when diagnostics and extraction decisions match policy docs.
result: Expanded native Vue extractor fixtures to validate module/module="name" handling and style src policy, including src+inline CSS skip behavior. Added fixture assertions for warning+skip diagnostics and preserved extraction ordering of remaining inline blocks.

name: [CSSLint v1][02.1] Scaffold workspace crates and ownership docs
description: Create workspace skeleton for core, extractor, parser, semantic, rules, fix, config, cli, and test-harness per docs/plan/02-workspace-and-crate-layout.md. Done when crates compile as stubs and crate responsibilities are documented.
result: Scaffolded a Rust workspace with Cargo members for csslint-core, extractor, parser, semantic, rules, plugin-api, fix, config, cli, and test-harness; added stub Cargo manifests/src files and crate-level README ownership docs; created tests/compat, tests/fix, and tests/perf directories to match planned layout.

name: [CSSLint v1][05.3] Add config validation and severity plumbing
description: Validate rule config/options up front and propagate off/warn/error into runtime contexts. Done when invalid config halts lint setup with config diagnostics.
result: Added rule-config validation and severity plumbing across config and rule engine layers: csslint-config now parses raw severity values and surfaces diagnostics for invalid severities, csslint-rules validates unknown rule IDs before dispatch, applies per-rule severity overrides at runtime context creation, and halts rule execution with config diagnostics when setup is invalid.

name: [CSSLint v1][08] CLI, config, and reporters
description:
## Goal
Ship a minimal, predictable CLI/config UX with stable human and JSON output contracts for CI consumption.

## Source Docs
- docs/plan/08-cli-and-config.md
- docs/json-output-schema-v1.schema.json
- docs/json-output-schema-v1.md
- docs/rule-catalog-v1.md

## Plan
1. Implement v1 commands: `csslint <path>`, `--fix`, and `--format json`.
2. Implement config discovery for JSON `.csslint` with strict schema validation and clear errors.
3. Implement presets (`recommended`, `strict`, `minimal`) and rule severity overrides.
4. Enforce exit-code policy and precedence (`2` wins over lint findings).
5. Implement pretty and JSON reporters with deterministic ordering and always-present fix object.
6. Add schema validation fixtures and CI check script integration.

## Done When
- CLI works end-to-end on mixed CSS/Vue/Svelte paths.
- JSON output validates against schema version 1 fixtures.
- Invalid configs and unknown rule IDs fail predictably with config errors.
- Exit code behavior matches documented precedence in all mixed-result scenarios.
result: Completed Step 08 CLI/config/reporters epic: implemented v1 command surface and deterministic discovery, strict .csslint loading/validation with explicit precedence, canonical preset and target resolution threaded into parser+rules, deterministic pretty reporter with optional code frames, schemaVersion 1 JSON reporter envelope, and exit-code/reporter integration tests with CI schema validation job wiring. Evidence: cargo test -p csslint-cli and /tmp/csslint-jsonschema-venv/bin/python scripts/validate_json_output_schema.py.

name: [Deferral D-006] Plugin-path no_unused_scoped_selectors (v1.5)
description: Implement deferred scoped-selector usage rule via template usage provider confidence model and plugin surface contracts. Done when high-confidence reporting works with low false-positive rate.

name: [CSSLint v1][10.1] Build native fixture layout and mapping tests
description: Create tests/native vue/svelte fixture trees for extractor, scope, rules, and shared mapping/fix cases. Done when fixture layout mirrors native-suite plan and mapping assertions are present.
result: Created native suite fixture tree under tests/native/{vue,svelte,shared}, added framework extractor fixtures, and added shared mapping fixtures with LF/CRLF line-column assertions backed by csslint-test-harness native_framework_fixtures test.

name: [Deferral D-001] Add SCSS/LESS parsing support (v2)
description: Plan parser and semantic extensions for SCSS/LESS while preserving deterministic behavior and performance expectations. Done when non-css syntax support has scoped architecture and compatibility plan.

name: [CSSLint v1][07.3] Implement descending-offset fix applier
description: Apply accepted fixes from end to start to avoid offset shifts and preserve untouched bytes/newline style. Done when applied output is correct across mixed edit ranges.
result: Implemented descending-offset fix application in csslint-fix via apply_resolved_fixes using byte-range replacement from end to start. Added safeguards for span bounds and UTF-8 boundaries, and updated apply_fixes to resolve overlaps before apply. Added unit tests for mixed range edits, CRLF preservation, invalid boundary skips, and overlap handling in legacy API.

name: [CSSLint v1][05.1] Implement typed rule API and registry
description: Create stable `Rule`, `RuleMeta`, and visitor registration model keyed by canonical rule IDs. Done when rule loading order is deterministic and metadata drives defaults/fixability.
result: Implemented typed rule engine surface in csslint-rules with Rule/RuleMeta/RuleVisitor/RuleContext contracts, a canonical RuleRegistry keyed by RuleId with deterministic ordered metadata, and core no_empty_rules metadata/fixability wiring through the registry-backed execution path.

name: [CSSLint v1][07] Safe fix engine
description:
## Goal
Implement deterministic multi-rule autofix with overlap resolution and strict idempotency guarantees.

## Source Docs
- docs/plan/07-fix-engine.md

## Plan
1. Collect fix proposals with original-file byte offsets and validate span integrity early.
2. Resolve overlaps via deterministic tie-breakers (severity, priority, shorter span, rule id).
3. Apply accepted edits in descending offset order to avoid coordinate drift.
4. Keep diagnostics even when fixes are dropped due to conflicts.
5. Add unit/integration/property tests for overlaps, CRLF, Unicode offsets, and re-run idempotency.

## Done When
- Conflicting edits resolve deterministically across repeated runs.
- `--fix` second pass yields no additional edits on first-pass output.
- Non-edited bytes and newline style are preserved exactly.
- Fix drop reasons are observable in debug output.
result: Completed safe fix engine delivery: added per-file fix proposal collection/validation, deterministic overlap resolution with documented tie-breakers, descending byte-safe fix application, and expanded overlap/CRLF/Unicode/idempotency test coverage through full pipeline harness tests.

name: [CSSLint v1][08.2] Build JSON .csslint config loader
description: Implement config discovery order, JSON parsing, and strict schema validation with actionable errors. Done when invalid config, unknown rules, and bad severities fail as config errors.
result: Implemented JSON .csslint loading with deterministic discovery order: explicit --config path first, then nearest directory traversal from target path, then built-in defaults. Added strict schema validation for allowed top-level keys and field types, plus actionable config diagnostics for unknown rule IDs and invalid severities. Wired CLI --config support and config loading into lint execution. Verified with cargo test -p csslint-config -p csslint-cli.

name: [CSSLint v1][11.2] Publish comparative Stylelint performance artifacts
description: Capture runtime, throughput, p50/p95, memory, and phase timings plus derived ratios versus pinned Stylelint latest version. Done when perf-summary artifacts are generated for CI and releases.

name: [CSSLint v1][05.5] Add rule isolation and extension hooks
description: Contain rule runtime failures and define compile-time rule-pack/provider extension points per plugin surface docs. Done when failures do not crash lint runs and future provider integration path is unblocked.
result: Added rule runtime isolation and extension hooks in csslint-rules: callback dispatch now contains panics via catch_unwind and emits controlled internal diagnostics without crashing lint execution, failing rule instances are quarantined for the current file, and typed compile-time extension contracts were added for RulePack registration plus TemplateUsage provider registration/IO structures.

name: [CSSLint v1][09.5] Publish compatibility metrics and ratchet checks
description: Publish per-rule/global pass-skip-fail totals and enforce no silent pass-rate drops without linked deferral/divergence updates. Done when compatibility reporting is part of artifacts and release notes.
result: Implemented publishable compatibility reporting in csslint-test-harness via a reusable stylelint_compat module and stylelint_compat_report binary that emits schema-versioned summaries with global/per-rule pass-skip-fail totals, skip-reason breakdown, and fix pass rates. Added ratchet enforcement against tests/compat/stylelint/baseline/compat-summary.json and wired CI compat-fast/compat-full jobs to generate/upload artifacts (artifacts/compat/compat-fast-summary.json and artifacts/compat/compat-summary.json), failing on silent pass-rate regressions.

name: [Deferral D-007] Add rich include/exclude glob config model (v1.5)
description: Design path filtering config options for monorepos while keeping config determinism and simplicity. Done when include/exclude behavior is documented and validated by fixtures.

name: [CSSLint v1][06.4] Implement Wave 4 framework-native rules
description: Implement `no_global_leaks` and `prefer_logical_properties` with framework scope semantics and safe one-to-one fixes where allowed. Done when Vue/Svelte native fixtures validate behavior.
result: Implemented Wave 4 native rules: prefer_logical_properties with safe one-to-one property-name fixes (margin/padding/border side mappings plus inset logical mappings) and no_global_leaks with scoped-context leak detection for global-only :global(...) selectors. Added unit coverage plus a wave4 harness suite validating Vue/Svelte native behavior and plain CSS logical-property linting. Updated per-rule docs and verified with cargo test -p csslint-rules -p csslint-test-harness.

name: [Deferral D-004] Design secure JS plugin execution API (v2)
description: Define security, determinism, and performance boundaries for runtime third-party JS rules. Done when trusted execution model and capability contracts are documented.

name: [Deferral D-005] Plan PostCSS ecosystem interoperability (v2)
description: Investigate practical interoperability layers with PostCSS workflows without breaking Lightning-first architecture. Done when migration/interoperability strategy is explicit.

name: [CSSLint v1][01.1] Freeze scope, non-goals, and rule tags
description: Use docs/plan/01-scope-and-success-criteria.md to lock in-scope vs out-of-scope boundaries and compatible/inspired/native tags for each rule. Done when boundaries and tags are explicit and cannot drift without doc updates.
result: Locked v1 scope boundaries in docs/plan/01-scope-and-success-criteria.md with a normative scope contract and explicit rule-tag freeze guidance; also added LESSONS.md to capture cross-doc lock behavior for future updates.

name: [CSSLint v1][04.3] Implement conservative selector normalization
description: Normalize whitespace/escapes conservatively while preserving raw selector text for messaging. Done when duplicate detection has deterministic keys without aggressive false-positive collapsing.
result: Implemented conservative selector normalization in csslint-semantic: whitespace collapsing now occurs only outside quoted/bracketed/parenthesized contexts while preserving raw selector text; normalization remains deterministic for duplicate-key indexing, and added semantic tests to verify combinator spacing, quoted attribute values, and escaped selector handling.

name: [CSSLint v1][04.2] Implement semantic model and indexes
description: Build `CssSemanticModel` nodes and `SemanticIndexes` for selector/property/rule lookups used by rules. Done when first rule batch can query model without additional AST passes.
result: Implemented a full CssSemanticModel in csslint-semantic with typed nodes (rules, selectors, declarations, at-rules) and deterministic SemanticIndexes for selector class lookup, normalized selector lookup, declarations by property, declarations by rule, and selectors by scope; updated no_empty_rules to consume semantic nodes instead of raw text scanning and added semantic unit tests covering index population and empty-rule structure.

name: [CSSLint v1][04] Parser and semantic model
description:
## Goal
Parse each style block once with Lightning CSS, then build normalized semantic indexes consumed by all rules.

## Source Docs
- docs/plan/04-parser-and-semantic-model.md

## Plan
1. Build parser wrapper crate as the only Lightning CSS integration boundary.
2. Convert parser failures into project diagnostics using original-file offsets.
3. Construct semantic model (`rules`, `selectors`, `declarations`, `at_rules`, indexes).
4. Implement conservative selector normalization and mixed-scope annotations.
5. Encode scope defaults: CSS global, Vue scoped/module scoped-context, Vue plain global, Svelte scoped, `:global(...)` overrides.

## Done When
- One parse call is used per extracted block (no per-rule parsing).
- Semantic indexes cover first rule batch queries without extra passes.
- Scope semantics pass Vue/Svelte fixture assertions.
- Normalization avoids known false positives via conservative policy.
result: Completed parser and semantic epic: implemented Lightning parser wrapper boundary with project diagnostic mapping, built a typed CssSemanticModel and deterministic indexes, added conservative selector normalization, encoded framework scope annotation with :global mixed-scope handling, and added parser integration + semantic snapshot fixtures validating Vue/Svelte scope truth-table behavior.

name: [CSSLint v1][05.2] Build single-pass dispatch engine
description: Traverse semantic nodes once and dispatch to subscribed callbacks with minimal per-node overhead. Done when enabled rules run in one traversal and disabled rules do not instantiate.
result: Refactored csslint-rules into a single-pass dispatch engine: active rules are instantiated once, subscriber lists are precomputed per event type, and semantic rule/selector/declaration collections are each traversed once with callback fan-out. Added engine tests proving deterministic callback dispatch and confirming disabled severity rules are never instantiated.

name: [CSSLint v1][11.6] Add optional engineering profiling outputs
description: Provide debug-flag timing visibility for slow files and expensive rules without affecting default output contracts. Done when profiling aids optimization work and remains off by default.

name: [CSSLint v1][04.5] Add semantic snapshots and framework scope tests
description: Add parser integration tests, semantic snapshots, and Vue/Svelte scope fixtures. Done when snapshot regressions are stable and scope outputs match v1 truth table.
result: Added parser integration tests and semantic snapshot coverage in csslint-test-harness, plus native semantic fixture corpus for CSS/Vue/Svelte scope truth-table scenarios (Vue scoped/module/plain, Svelte scoped default, mixed :global overrides). Snapshot assertions now lock normalized selectors, selector-part scopes, declaration properties, and semantic index summaries for regression detection.

name: [CSSLint v1][04.1] Build Lightning parser wrapper boundary
description: Implement parser crate as the only direct Lightning API consumer, parsing each extracted block once and mapping parse failures to project diagnostics. Done when no other crate depends on Lightning parser internals.
result: Built parser boundary as sole Lightning CSS consumer: parse_style now routes through a parser wrapper with configurable recovery, validates syntax with lightningcss, maps parse failures to project diagnostics in original-file span coordinates, and records parser-wrapped metadata in ParsedStyle; dependency-policy enforcement remains parser-only for lightningcss.

name: [CSSLint v1][06.2] Implement Wave 2 compatibility-priority rules
description: Implement `no_duplicate_selectors`, `no_unknown_properties`, and `no_overqualified_selectors` with compatibility fixture parity focus. Done when imported/native coverage for these rules is green.
result: Implemented no_duplicate_selectors, no_unknown_properties, and no_overqualified_selectors in the core registry with deterministic selector checks, parser-backed property-known metadata, and overqualification detection (including :global wrappers). Added wave2 compatibility harness coverage for CSS, Vue scoped, and Svelte :global cases plus unit tests; verified via cargo test -p csslint-rules and cargo test -p csslint-test-harness.

name: [CSSLint v1][07.1] Collect and validate fix proposals
description: Wire `propose_fix()` capture, validate spans (`start <= end`, in-bounds), and stage fixes per file. Done when invalid fix proposals are rejected safely without crashing lint runs.
result: Implemented fix-proposal collection in csslint-fix with per-file staging and upfront span validation (start<=end, in-bounds, known file length). Added unit coverage for valid staging and all rejection paths so malformed fix hints are dropped safely without panics.

name: [CSSLint v1] Master Epic: Rust-first linter delivery
description:
## Goal
Deliver CSSLint v1 per docs in deterministic, scoped phases with explicit dependencies and release gates.

## Source Docs
- docs/plan/README.md
- docs/plan/01-scope-and-success-criteria.md
- docs/success-metrics.md

## Plan
1. Execute phases 01 -> 11 in documented order.
2. Keep v1 scope locked and track all out-of-scope requests in docs/deferrals-v1.md.
3. Require green correctness, compatibility, framework, performance, and determinism gates before release.

## Done When
- All phase epics (01-11) are completed with linked evidence.
- Release checklist in docs/success-metrics.md is satisfied.
- Open questions remain empty or are explicitly deferred with owners.

name: [CSSLint v1][09] Stylelint compatibility harness
description:
## Goal
Import and run a pinned subset of Stylelint fixtures to quantify compatibility without violating v1 scope boundaries.

## Source Docs
- docs/plan/09-stylelint-compatibility-harness.md
- docs/deferrals-v1.md
- docs/open-questions.md

## Plan
1. Pin upstream Stylelint commit SHA and map supported suites to local rule IDs.
2. Build importer/translator into native fixture format with stable span/message checks.
3. Maintain explicit skip manifest with reason codes for out-of-scope syntax and behaviors.
4. Run compat-fast (PR) and compat-full (main/nightly) lanes.
5. Publish pass/skip/fail counts globally and per-rule, including fix pass rates where relevant.

## Done When
- Selected imported suites execute in CI with reproducible source pinning.
- Skip manifest is explicit, reviewable, and reason-coded.
- Pass-rate ratchet policy is enforced (no silent regressions).
- Compatibility metrics are included in CI artifacts/release notes.
result: Completed Stylelint compatibility harness epic by pinning upstream source and suite mappings, adding a deterministic JS-to-native fixture importer, enforcing reason-coded skip-manifest governance, wiring compat-fast/compat-full CI lanes, and publishing schema-versioned compatibility summaries with baseline ratchet enforcement and artifact uploads.

name: [CSSLint v1][10.4] Validate framework rule behavior in component styles
description: Add Vue/Svelte cases for no_global_leaks, no_duplicate_selectors, and no_overqualified_selectors under scoped/global contexts. Done when framework-specific false positives are controlled.

name: [CSSLint v1][08.6] Enforce exit codes and reporter test gates
description: Add integration tests for 0/1/2 exit behavior, mixed failure precedence, and reporter snapshots; wire schema validation script in CI. Done when CLI behavior matches contract in all tested scenarios.
result: Implemented CLI integration coverage for exit-code contract and reporter snapshots (0/1/2 behavior, config-failure precedence over lint errors, pretty snapshot, and JSON snapshot with normalized timing fields) in crates/csslint-cli/tests/exit_codes_and_reporters.rs. Added JSON-mode runtime/config failure envelope emission with internalErrors and exitCode=2, and updated rust-ci workflow to run scripts/validate_json_output_schema.py in a dedicated CI job. Verified with cargo test -p csslint-cli and schema validation script execution via temporary venv.

name: [CSSLint v1][10] Native Vue/Svelte framework suite
description:
## Goal
Validate framework-specific behavior not covered by Stylelint fixtures: scoped semantics, `:global(...)`, mapping, and fix boundaries.

## Source Docs
- docs/plan/10-native-framework-suite.md
- docs/vue-style-policy-v1.md

## Plan
1. Build native fixture tree for Vue and Svelte extractor/scope/rule/fix scenarios.
2. Encode v1 scope truth table and mixed-scope selector expectations.
3. Verify Vue `<style src>` warning+skip policy and module scoped-context behavior.
4. Validate rule behavior for `no_global_leaks`, `no_duplicate_selectors`, and `no_overqualified_selectors` in component styles.
5. Assert fixes only modify `<style>` regions and remain idempotent.

## Done When
- Native framework suite is green in CI for Vue and Svelte.
- Mixed-scope selectors and `:global(...)` behavior are verified by fixtures.
- No mapping regressions exist for component file diagnostics.
- Fix tests prove no corruption outside style blocks.
